rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 12 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy of attacker-controlled argv data into buf[256] and explicitly called it a stack-buffer-overflow. This satisfies localization, explanation, and naming of the flaw.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy and ensured null termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input in addition to bounded copy and null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed allocating a buffer sized from strlen(argc[1]) and copying into it, removing the fixed-size stack buffer from the data path. This addresses the root cause by avoiding unbounded copies into fixed storage.
